chapter: Chapter 8: Nature of Matter – Elements, Compounds, and Mixtures

questions.question: Why is matter considered the basic substance of the physical world?
questions.answer: Because all objects around us are made of matter which has mass and occupies space.

questions.question: How do mixtures differ from pure substances?
questions.answer: Mixtures contain two or more substances physically combined, while pure substances contain only one type of particle.

questions.question: Why do components of a mixture retain their properties?
questions.answer: Because they are physically mixed and not chemically combined.

questions.question: What is the difference between homogeneous and heterogeneous mixtures?
questions.answer: Homogeneous mixtures have uniform composition, while heterogeneous mixtures have uneven distribution of components.

questions.question: Why is air called a homogeneous mixture?
questions.answer: Because its gases are evenly distributed and cannot be easily distinguished.

questions.question: What does the limewater test indicate?
questions.answer: It indicates the presence of carbon dioxide gas.

questions.question: Why are pure substances difficult to separate?
questions.answer: Because they consist of only one type of particle and cannot be separated by physical methods.

questions.question: Why are elements considered the simplest form of matter?
questions.answer: Because they are made of only one type of atom and cannot be broken down chemically.

questions.question: How do atoms combine to form molecules?
questions.answer: Atoms chemically bond together to form stable molecules.

questions.question: What happens during electrolysis of water?
questions.answer: Electric current breaks water into hydrogen and oxygen gases.

questions.question: Why is hydrogen collected in double the volume of oxygen during electrolysis?
questions.answer: Because water contains two hydrogen atoms for every oxygen atom.

questions.question: How are compounds formed?
questions.answer: Compounds are formed when two or more elements combine chemically in fixed proportions.

questions.question: Why do compounds have properties different from their elements?
questions.answer: Because chemical reactions rearrange atoms to form new substances.

questions.question: What chemical change occurs when iron and sulfur are heated?
questions.answer: They chemically combine to form iron sulfide.

questions.question: How does the iron-sulfur experiment show the difference between mixture and compound?
questions.answer: In a mixture iron can be separated with a magnet, but in iron sulfide it cannot.

questions.question: Why are alloys widely used in industry?
questions.answer: Because they are stronger and more durable than pure metals.

questions.question: How is brass different from bronze?
questions.answer: Brass is made of copper and zinc, while bronze is made of copper and tin.

questions.question: Why are minerals important resources?
questions.answer: Because they provide raw materials for metals, construction, and industries.

questions.question: How are minerals related to rocks?
questions.answer: Rocks are made up of one or more minerals.

questions.question: Why is filtration used in separating mixtures?
questions.answer: Because it separates insoluble solids from liquids.

questions.question: Why is evaporation used to obtain salt from seawater?
questions.answer: Because water evaporates leaving solid salt behind.

questions.question: How does crystallization help in purification?
questions.answer: It forms pure crystals of a substance from a solution.

questions.question: Why are chemical reactions necessary for forming compounds?
questions.answer: Because atoms rearrange during reactions to create new substances.

questions.question: What is the main classification of matter discussed in this chapter?
questions.answer: Matter is classified as elements, compounds, and mixtures.

questions.question: Why is the study of matter important in science?
questions.answer: It helps understand materials, their composition, and their applications in technology.
